doc.title: PTFE (Polytetrafluoroethylen) – Engineering- und RAG-Referenz (v1.2)
doc.upgrade_note: v1.2: Permeation normative anchors, hardened UHP gate, additional UHP/permeation factcards, compound decision matrix (separate JSON).

S1.title: TEKU GmbH – PTFE fluoroplastic: Properties and characteristic data (EN_Fluoroplastics_s.pdf)

S3.title: ScienceDirect Topics – Poly(tetrafluoroethylene) overview (tables on permeation; book excerpts incl. McKeen 2012)

S4.title: ASTM D1434-82(2015)e1 – Determining Gas Permeability Characteristics of Plastic Film and Sheeting

S5.title: ASTM D4745 – Filled PTFE molding compounds classification system (summary page)

S6.title: Bal Seal Engineering – Chemical Compatibility Guide TR-60A (compound notes incl. glass-filled abrasive; polymer-filled for soft metals; semiconductor note)

S7.title: Chemours / Teflon™ – PTFE product info (paste extrusion; lubricant removal 100–300°C; sintering ~342°C)

S8.title: pro-kunststoff.de – Introduction into the processing of PTFE resins (Feb 2020)

S9.title: CRP Technology – Managing Permeation on PTFE lined Piping in Chlorine & ... (NACE webinar basis)

S10.title: CDC/NIOSH – Decomposition products of fluorocarbon polymers (PTFE) (stacks.cdc.gov PDF)

S11.title: Dolatabad et al. 2025 – Thermal decomposition of fluoropolymers (ScienceDirect abstract)

S12.title: Chemours – Teflon™ fluoropolymers and the semiconductor industry brochure (PDF)

S13.title: Klaas et al. 2005 – The tribological behaviour of glass filled PTFE (Tribology International)

S14.title: Bahadur 1984 – The wear of filled polytetrafluoroethylene (Wear)

S15.title: ASTM D543 – Chemical Resistance of Plastics (overview)

S16.title: ISO 15105-1 — Plastics — Film and sheeting — Determination of gas-transmission rate — Part 1: Differential-pressure methods

S17.title: ISO 15105-2 — Plastics — Film and sheeting — Determination of gas-transmission rate — Part 2: Equal-pressure method

S18.title: SEMI F57 — Polymer Components Used in Ultrapure Water and Liquid Chemical Distribution Systems (standard, licensed)

S19.title: Industry UHP fluid handling surface finish practice (Ra requirements; vendor-/fab-specific qualification specs)

factcards.value: (–CF2–CF2–)n
factcards.conditions: Linear, unbranched fluoropolymer; carbon backbone shielded by fluorine helix
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer melt-processability from being a thermoplastic; PTFE has extremely high melt viscosity and is typically processed by sintering.

factcards.value: partly crystalline (semi-crystalline)
factcards.conditions: Properties depend strongly on crystallinity and processing history (sinter + cooling)
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume a single crystallinity value without DSC/XRD data and the exact grade/process.

factcards.conditions: Crystallite melting range referenced for PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not treat 327°C as a safe operating limit; it is the melting transition, not a continuous service rating.

factcards.conditions: Common engineering guideline for continuous use; must be validated per grade, stress and environment
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not guarantee long-term performance at 260°C without creep/load and media-specific validation.

factcards.conditions: Breakdown products must be expected above ~300°C per manufacturer guidance
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer exact toxic species or rates without a controlled decomposition study and temperature history.

factcards.conditions: Hydrogen fluoride reported released when PTFE heated above 300°C in decomposition studies
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume HF is the only hazard; particulate and other fluorinated products can dominate toxicity depending on conditions.

factcards.conditions: Peer-reviewed review notes C–F bond cleavage begins above ~400°C with release of TFE and radicals
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not extrapolate decomposition chemistry to normal service temperatures (<300°C).

factcards.value: cryogenic-capable
factcards.conditions: PTFE remains usable at very low temperatures; design must consider stiffness increase and seal energization
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer elastomer-like resilience at cryogenic temperatures; PTFE is not elastic and needs mechanical energization for seals.

factcards.value: undetectable / very low
factcards.conditions: Manufacturer guidance: water absorption essentially undetectable
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer zero outgassing; low bulk absorption does not eliminate surface-adsorbed water or volatiles.

factcards.value: very low
factcards.conditions: Low surface energy drives non-stick behavior and low adhesion
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer low friction under all loads/speeds; wear can still be high depending on counterface and PV.

factcards.value: Resistant to many aggressive acids/bases/solvents
factcards.conditions: Examples: HCl, HF, fuming H2SO4/HNO3, hot NaOH, Cl2, H2O2, esters, ketones, alcohols, acid chlorides
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer compatibility at any temperature/concentration/phase; always require explicit conditions.

factcards.value: susceptible near ~327°C
factcards.conditions: Highly fluorinated oils can attack/swell PTFE close to crystallite melting range
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer chemical attack at room temperature from this; the effect is temperature-dependent.

factcards.value: reversible swelling
factcards.conditions: At room temperature, fluorine-containing hydrocarbons can swell PTFE reversibly
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer permanent damage from swelling without dimensional/weight-change data and recovery check.

factcards.value: attacked in molten or dissolved form
factcards.conditions: Molten/dissolved alkali metals attack PTFE; brown discoloration; fluorine extracted from molecule
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that aqueous alkali solutions behave like molten alkali metals; phase and temperature are decisive.

factcards.value: resistance deteriorates at high temperature
factcards.conditions: At high temperatures, resistance to chlorine trifluoride deteriorates
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer safe use with ClF3 at any temperature; require explicit compatibility validation and safety review.

factcards.value: resistance deteriorates at high temperature
factcards.conditions: At high temperatures, resistance to elemental fluorine deteriorates
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer safe use in fluorination service without specialized materials selection and testing.

factcards.value: Not recommended (C)
factcards.conditions: PTFE sealing products rated 'C' for chlorine trifluoride in TEADIT chart
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not treat a single vendor chart as universal truth; use as a red-flag requiring deeper validation.

factcards.value: Not recommended (C)
factcards.conditions: PTFE sealing products rated 'C' for molten alkali metals in TEADIT chart
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume PTFE failure mode; different compounds and hardware can shift behavior but hazard remains extreme.

factcards.value: Chemical resistance ≠ impermeability
factcards.conditions: Even chemically inert PTFE can allow permeation of gases/vapors; permeation depends on thickness, temperature, gradient, morphology
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer 'no permeation' from 'inert'. Always consider permeation in lined steel, vacuum, UHP systems.

factcards.value: GTR, permeance, permeability
factcards.conditions: D1434 defines gas transmission rate, permeance, permeability; permeability meaningful for homogeneous materials
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not compare permeability values unless test conditions (T, Δp, humidity) and units match.

factcards.conditions: SO2 at 23°C; Dyneon TFM 1700 PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not apply to other grades, thicknesses, or temperatures without correction; values are grade- and condition-specific.

factcards.conditions: SO2 at 23°C; Dyneon TF 1750 PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer ranking between materials unless same method/conditions are used.

factcards.conditions: HCl gas at 54°C; Dyneon TFM 1700 PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume HCl resistance implies zero permeation; corrosion can occur behind liners.

factcards.conditions: HCl gas at 54°C; Dyneon TF 1750 PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that a lower permeability necessarily means lower corrosion risk; defects and bonding dominate in practice.

factcards.conditions: Chlorine gas at 54°C; Dyneon TFM 1700 PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that chlorine gas is 'blocked' by PTFE; permeation is non-zero.

factcards.conditions: Water vapor at 20°C; Dyneon TFM 1700 PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not extrapolate to vacuum bakeout; surface adsorption and diffusion kinetics matter.

factcards.value: blistering + corrosion of steel
factcards.conditions: Permeation through PTFE liners can cause blistering of PTFE and corrosion of steel substrate; increases maintenance and risk
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer the liner has failed chemically; permeation is a transport phenomenon even without polymer attack.

factcards.value: used to reduce contamination/corrosion/leaching
factcards.conditions: Chemours brochure: fluoropolymers chemically inert and protect against ionic/metallic contamination in fab fluid handling
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer PTFE automatically meets UHP specs; grade, additives, cleaning, extractables and particle shedding must be qualified.

factcards.value: lubricant removal then sintering
factcards.conditions: Chemours: lubricant removal typically 100–300°C; sintering above melting point (~342°C) to produce void-free PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer an article is void-free without density/porosity checks; cycle control is necessary but not sufficient.

factcards.value: boiling point depends on wall thickness
factcards.conditions: Processing guide: thin-walled/films use high-boiling lubricants; thick-walled/liners prefer low-boiling
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer lubricant choice from geometry alone; extrusion method, removal route, and contamination limits also govern.

factcards.value: must absorb readily + be removed easily; must not discolor during sintering
factcards.conditions: Processing guide requirement for lubricant
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer a lubricant is acceptable for UHP without extractables/ionic contamination data.

factcards.value: preform pressure + sintering time/temperature + cooling rate
factcards.conditions: Chemours PTFE granular product info: final properties depend on preform pressure, sintering time/temperature, cooling rate
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not compare mechanical properties across vendors without matching cycles and test standards.

factcards.value: solid-solid transition ~19°C (important for dimensional stability)
factcards.conditions: Processing guide notes crystalline transition around ~19°C affecting volume
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not claim a specific expansion jump without measuring the specific grade; transition range can vary.

factcards.value: strong tendency to creep (cold flow)
factcards.conditions: PTFE exhibits significant creep; design needs preload/energization and creep allowance
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer creep rate without stress, temperature and time; requires creep curves for the specific grade/compound.

factcards.value: abrasion resistance of unfilled PTFE is low
factcards.conditions: Manufacturer guidance: abrasion resistance of PTFE is low; compounded PTFE improves abrasion
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer absolute wear rate; wear depends on PV, counterface, lubrication and debris.

factcards.value: PTFE compounds show good abrasion resistance at relatively high loads
factcards.conditions: General statement for PTFE compounds
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume all compounds improve wear; filler selection can worsen wear vs certain counterfaces.

factcards.value: low friction; very good chemical resistance; FDA compliant; light-duty service
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer suitability for high PV dynamic sealing from 'low friction'; wear and creep can dominate.

factcards.value: glass-fiber-filled PTFE with lubricant: good wear resistance but abrasive to counter surface
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend glass-filled against soft shafts without hardness and surface finish confirmation.

factcards.value: polymer-filled PTFE recommended for low-to-high speed against soft metals
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer universal chemical compatibility; some polymer-filled grades are not suitable with strong acids/high pH.

factcards.value: some polymer-filled PTFE not suitable with strong acids and high pH liquids
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that 'polymer-filled' implies chemical resistance equal to virgin PTFE; fill system can reduce compatibility.

factcards.value: MoS2-filled PTFE noted for vacuum/inert gas service; excellent wear; high extrusion resistance
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer semiconductor compatibility; MoS2 can be a contamination concern depending on spec.

factcards.value: PTFE compound noted as suitable for semiconductor applications (low permeability/deformation)
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not treat as a generic PTFE property; this is grade-specific and still requires fab qualification.

factcards.value: classification system for filled PTFE molding compounds
factcards.conditions: D4745 covers filled PTFE molding compounds based on virgin PTFE resins (with certain exclusions); defines types
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer a compound meets D4745 unless supplier certifies and test results match.

factcards.value: 93 vs 1
factcards.conditions: Table shows helium gas permeation: PTFE vs Teflon NXT at 2 mm: 93 vs 1 (units per table)
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not use as absolute without the original table context (temperature, method, units). Use as evidence that modified PTFE can reduce permeation.

factcards.conditions: Oxygen at 20°C; Dyneon TFM 1700
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not extrapolate across temperature; permeability rises strongly with temperature.

factcards.conditions: CO2 at 20°C; Dyneon TFM 1700
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not compare to other materials unless same test standard/conditions.

factcards.conditions: N2 at 20°C; Dyneon TFM 1700
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume nitrogen is negligible; in vacuum systems, even small permeation can set base pressure.

factcards.value: entrapped volatiles / permeant gas + temperature
factcards.conditions: Blistering can result from permeation and from trapped volatiles (lubricant not removed) during sinter
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not attribute blistering to 'chemical attack' without confirming media, temperature, and internal pressure mechanisms.

factcards.value: brown discoloration indicates alkali-metal attack extracting fluorine
factcards.conditions: Observed when PTFE attacked by molten/dissolved alkali metals
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not generalize brown color to chemical attack in all cases; overheating can also discolor; require context.

factcards.value: sinter above melt point to consolidate void-free structure
factcards.conditions: Chemours: sintering above melting point (~342°C) produces void-free solid PTFE resin
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer void-free without density/CT/ultrasound; process claims are not proof.

factcards.value: filler choice must match counterface hardness/finish
factcards.conditions: Abrasive fillers (e.g., glass fiber) can damage soft metals; polymer-filled options can reduce abrasion
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend a filler without knowing counterface material/hardness and lubrication regime.

factcards.value: UHP/semicon forbids uncontrolled fillers/additives/metal ions
factcards.conditions: Fab fluid handling targets low ionic/metallic contamination; material grade + cleaning matter
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer purity from polymer family name; request grade (UHP/HP), certification, extractables data.

factcards.value: PFIB identified among decomposition products at ~380–400°C and above
factcards.conditions: CDC report summarizing literature; PFIB is highly toxic
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not claim PFIB generation in normal service; it is a decomposition product requiring overheating conditions.

factcards.value: particulate fraction can dominate toxicity; filtration reduced lethality in animal studies
factcards.conditions: CDC report: filtering pyrolysis products removed toxicity in some studies
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer safe handling without proper industrial hygiene; use established safety protocols for overheating events.

factcards.value: Not recommended for many PTFE grades
factcards.conditions: TEADIT chart lists some HF cases (e.g., anhydrous HF) as C for several PTFE gasket products; temperature dependent
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not generalize HF compatibility; require concentration, temperature, and product/grade.

factcards.value: Consult (B)
factcards.conditions: TEADIT chart lists iodine pentafluoride as B (consult)
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer compatibility; treat B as hard gate requiring specialist review.

factcards.value: lubricant must not cause decolouration during sintering
factcards.conditions: Processing guide requirement
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not attribute discoloration to lubricant without checking temperature overshoot and oxygen exposure.

factcards.conditions: Lubricant removal step typically in 100–300°C range
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer full removal without mass-loss or residual analysis; thick sections require time and venting.

factcards.conditions: Sinter step to melt resin above melting point; vendor datasheet quotes ~342°C
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume 342°C is universal; PTFE melt range depends on grade; always follow supplier profile.

factcards.value: susceptible near melting range
factcards.conditions: Highly fluorinated oils can attack PTFE near 327°C
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer attack at lower temperatures; specify actual oil identity and temperature profile.

factcards.conditions: Water vapor at 80°C; Dyneon TFM 1700
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not extrapolate linearly; diffusion is temperature-activated and non-linear.

factcards.value: PTFE seals often require energizers / preload
factcards.conditions: Because PTFE is not elastomeric and creeps, sealing needs spring-energized or controlled compression
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that a static compression set metric exists like elastomers; PTFE behaves differently.

factcards.value: Always request: media, concentration, temperature, phase, pressure, time, stress
factcards.conditions: Hard gate for any 'compatibility' answer
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not provide unconditional compatibility statements.

factcards.value: high chemical resistance; moderate duty service
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer higher wear resistance than all other fillers; performance is application-specific.

factcards.value: good wear resistance in liquids and humid conditions
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer good dry-run performance from humid/liquid rating.

factcards.value: polyimide-filled PTFE designed for gas compressor/oxygen intensifier systems; FDA compliant; extremely low seal wear
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not declare oxygen-service safe solely based on a compound label; perform oxygen compatibility and ignition risk assessment (pressure, velocity, contaminants).

factcards.value: use modified PTFE / filled systems to reduce permeability and deformation
factcards.conditions: Bal Seal TA compound description: low permeability/deformation; suitable for semiconductor; good finishes
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that all modified PTFE has low permeability; request permeation test data for target media.

factcards.value: wear depends on ability to form adherent transfer film on counterface
factcards.conditions: Dry sliding and water-lubricated tests: wear dependent on transfer film formation
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer wear rate from filler name only; transfer-film formation depends on counterface roughness, material and environment.

factcards.value: glass bead filled shows thicker films + higher wear vs other glass forms
factcards.conditions: 25 wt% glass beads vs fibers/flakes; dry and water tests
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not generalize across bead type (hollow vs solid) and test conditions; require filler morphology data.

factcards.value: glass fibers and solid beads showed lowest wear; hollow beads highest
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 test range 2.6–6.4 MPa; v~0.2 m/s
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not apply these rankings to lubricated oil, abrasive slurry, or different PV; verify in application-relevant tests.

factcards.value: adding lamellar solid lubricant to glass fiber reduced wear and friction
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 observation
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer which lubricant (MoS2 vs graphite) is best without considering purity, environment and counterface.

factcards.value: XPS showed presence of metal fluoride on metal counterface
factcards.conditions: Tribochemical effects in PTFE/stainless steel wear couples
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer corrosion risk without environment details; tribochemical fluorination is a surface phenomenon during sliding.

factcards.value: banded structure with crystalline slices separated by thin amorphous regions
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 cites PTFE banded morphology; linked to wear debris formation
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer slice thickness or morphology without microscopy; structure varies by grade and processing.

factcards.value: unfilled PTFE wear rate often quoted ~1e-3 mm^3/(N·m)
factcards.conditions: Literature value referenced in Klaas 2005 introduction
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not use as design value; wear rates vary orders of magnitude with PV, roughness, debris and environment.

factcards.value: wear can improve up to ~3 orders of magnitude with suitable fillers
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 overview statement
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer a specific improvement factor without identifying filler type, loading and test method.

factcards.value: standard practice for evaluating chemical resistance of plastics (exposure + property change)
factcards.conditions: Chemical resistance testing framework (mass/size/property change after exposure)
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer compatibility from polymer family name; require test or credible compatibility data for target conditions.

factcards.value: permeability increases with temperature
factcards.conditions: Diffusion activated; tables show higher water vapor transmission at 80°C vs 20°C
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not extrapolate linearly; use Arrhenius-style fits or measured data if high accuracy is required.

factcards.value: vacuum/inert gas service stresses permeation and outgassing; use low-permeability grades
factcards.conditions: Bal Seal lists certain PTFE compounds for vacuum service; permeation data needed
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer vacuum suitability from 'chemically inert'; quantify permeation/outgassing and particle shedding.

factcards.value: fill systems can reduce compatibility vs virgin PTFE
factcards.conditions: Example: polymer-filled PTFE not suitable with strong acids/high pH per Bal Seal guide
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not treat compatibility as solely polymer-driven; consider filler chemistry and binder/additives.

factcards.value: bulk chemical distribution; wet etch/strip/clean; CMP; wafer handling
factcards.conditions: Chemours brochure use-cases for fluoropolymers in semiconductor fabs
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer the same material is suitable for all fab chemistries; verify with process chemical list and specs.

factcards.value: pyrolysis particulate reported ~0.2–0.5 µm; 99% <2 µm in one study
factcards.conditions: CDC report summarizing fume particulate sizes and inhalable fraction
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer exposure levels; this is particle-size characterization, not workplace concentration.

factcards.value: PTFE composites in study were compression molded and free sintered to billets
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 materials preparation
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer your product is equivalent unless processing route and cycle are similar.

factcards.value: modified PTFE grades (e.g., TFM, NXT) show lower permeability for some gases than conventional PTFE
factcards.conditions: Tables compare TFM vs TF; NXT vs PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer 'modified' always better; request specific permeation dataset for target gas and temperature.

factcards.value: residual lubricant/volatiles can create voids, blisters, porosity during heating
factcards.conditions: Lubricant must be removed before sintering; thick sections are high risk
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not diagnose without process log; ask for thermal profile, mass loss, and cross-section microscopy.

factcards.value: cloudy/opaque PTFE can indicate voids, insufficient sintering, or filler dispersion issues
factcards.conditions: Common troubleshooting; confirm by density, microscopy, CT
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not equate 'cloudy' with 'bad' for filled PTFE; fillers can make material opaque by design.

factcards.value: excellent weather resistance (incl. ozone/UV)
factcards.conditions: Manufacturer guidance: PTFE resists aging/weather under extreme climate
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer absence of mechanical degradation under combined stress (UV + load + temperature); validate if high-stress outdoor application.

factcards.value: excellent electrical insulation; low dielectric loss
factcards.conditions: Fluoropolymers used as insulators; PTFE notable
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer dielectric performance at high frequency/temperature without datasheet for the exact grade.

factcards.value: low friction attributed to low shear transfer film; fluorine shields backbone
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 discussion of mechanism
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer COF constant; COF depends on counterface, load, speed, and environment.

factcards.value: permeation tests must state conditioning and test conditions (T, humidity, Δp)
factcards.conditions: ASTM D1434 requires stating temperature, partial pressure and other factors like humidity
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not compare results across labs without explicit conditioning and test cell details.

factcards.value: gas sealing limited by permeation and creep; consider energizers and low-permeability grades
factcards.conditions: Combine creep tendency and permeation data
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not promise leak-tightness for gases without leak-rate specs and test at pressure/temperature cycles.

factcards.value: at high sliding speed, frictional heating and poor thermal conductivity can drive wear; fillers can improve heat conduction
factcards.conditions: General tribology principle; fillers like metals/carbon used for thermal conduction
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer safe PV limit; require application PV, cooling, and validated wear tests.

factcards.value: metal-filled PTFE can lose chemical resistance if filler corrodes (e.g., acids/alkalis attack metals)
factcards.conditions: General material-selection caution; verify filler chemistry
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend bronze/steel-filled PTFE in corrosive media without a filler-specific compatibility review.

factcards.value: PTFE generally resistant to many oxidizers (e.g., H2O2, fuming acids) at many conditions
factcards.conditions: Vendor list includes oxidizers; depends on temperature
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer compatibility with halogen fluorides (ClF3, BrF3) or elemental fluorine; treat as special-case killers.

factcards.value: halogen gases can permeate PTFE; behind-liner corrosion possible
factcards.conditions: Chlorine permeability values + lined equipment issues
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that polymer survives means equipment is safe; evaluate substrate, venting, and detection.

factcards.value: permeance relates to thickness; permeability is bulk property; increasing thickness reduces GTR at fixed permeability
factcards.conditions: ASTM D1434 definitions
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer thicker is always better; thicker liners increase thermal gradients and can trap permeant leading to blistering.

factcards.value: transfer film growth involves mechanical interlocking into asperity valleys; roughness matters
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 observation
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer that rougher is always better; too rough increases abrasion and wear.

factcards.value: wear mechanisms differ in dry vs water-lubricated sliding
factcards.conditions: Klaas 2005 performed both; rankings can shift
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not extrapolate dry test results to aqueous service without validation.

factcards.value: any quantitative property must include units + method + conditions
factcards.conditions: RAG hard rule
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not generate numbers from memory or inference.

factcards.value: vendor compatibility charts are rank-2; treat A/B/C as guidance, not certification
factcards.conditions: Use TEADIT/Bal Seal as red-flag layer; confirm with rank-1 tests/standards for high-risk
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not treat A-rating as a guarantee for your exact compound, stress state, and temperature.

factcards.value: oxygen service requires ignition risk assessment; material label alone insufficient
factcards.conditions: Bal Seal mentions oxygen intensifier systems; oxygen compatibility is system-level
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend for oxygen > certain pressures/velocities without oxygen compatibility protocol and cleanliness controls.

factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff guide: PTFE virgin resin melting point about 342°C; above becomes translucent
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not treat 342°C as universal; melting behavior depends on grade and measurement (DSC vs sintered).

factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff guide: sintered finished goods show melting point 327°C
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume your component matches this unless processed similarly; DSC recommended.

factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff guide: virgin resin crystallinity range
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer finished part crystallinity from resin value; processing reduces crystallinity substantially.

factcards.conditions: Void-free finished products depending on processing conditions; corresponds to crystallinity 50–70% per guide
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not use density alone to certify void-free; use microscopy/CT and compare to reference.

factcards.conditions: Important crystalline transition at ~19°C (triclinic→hexagonal) with ~1% volume increase
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not ignore in precision fits; for tight tolerances, require stabilization or design clearance across 10–30°C.

factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff summary of application temperature range
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer mechanical performance constant over this range; modulus and creep change strongly with temperature.

factcards.value: very high purity; without additives
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff summary: PTFE very high purity; without additives
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer 'additive-free' for all commercial grades; some grades may contain processing aids or fillers; request certification.

factcards.value: PPVE (perfluoropropyl vinyl ether) comonomer (small amount)
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff: modified PTFE contains small amount of fully fluorinated modifier PPVE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer equivalence between all 'modified PTFE' brands; comonomer content and effects vary.

factcards.value: reduced vs standard PTFE
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff: modified PTFE can reduce porosity, permeation, and strong cold-flow
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume modified PTFE solves permeation; still requires permeation data for target gas/temperature.

factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff: defined sintering cycle with max 370–380°C; above 342°C PTFE becomes amorphous and particles sinter
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not exceed temperature without decomposition risk review; monitor oven uniformity and oxygen exposure.

factcards.value: compaction must allow incorporated air to escape
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff: compacting serves to compress powder and allow air to escape
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume void-free if compaction/dwell time not controlled; trapped air is a primary void mechanism.

factcards.value: store ~12–15°C before processing
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff: recommended to store paste-PTFE below transition point 19°C to prevent clumping; cooling can take >35h for 25kg drum
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer your resin is conditioned; request handling history and storage temperatures.

factcards.value: avoid strong mechanical strain during processing to avoid voids
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff caution: polymer powder sensitive; strong mechanical strain can cause voids
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not diagnose without knowing processing route (ram extrusion vs compression) and shear history.

factcards.value: modified PTFE main applications include chemical engineering, semiconductor, biotechnology
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff: application note for modified PTFE
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer automatic fab qualification; this is a general application statement.

factcards.value: less cold flow; less creep especially at higher temperature/load
factcards.conditions: Pro-kunststoff: summary of modified PTFE improvements
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not infer magnitude without the original curves and load/time conditions.

factcards.value: Test methods differ (differential pressure vs isostatic/equal-pressure carrier-gas setups)
factcards.conditions: Gas permeability / transmission testing; methodology influences comparability of values
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not directly compare permeability/transmission values across different ISO/ASTM methods without acknowledging methodological offsets and units.

factcards.value: SEMI F57 is a key qualification reference for ultra-pure polymer components
factcards.conditions: UPW and liquid chemical distribution systems; exact acceptance depends on component class and test scope
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not claim UHP suitability without documented SEMI F57 compliance (or equivalent extractables/particles test evidence).

factcards.value: low ppb to ppt range
factcards.conditions: UHP PTFE components after controlled extraction (e.g., hot UPW or dilute acid); exact analyte list and limits are spec-dependent
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not state a component is 'metal-free'; request analyte-specific ICP-MS extractables data for the wetted surface and the relevant fab spec.

factcards.value: <0.25 (often tighter, application-specific)
factcards.conditions: Typical UHP fluid-handling practice; exact finish requirement is fab/vendor/spec specific and may be lower (e.g., <0.15 µm)
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend standard skived/machined PTFE for UHP without specifying surface finishing route, cleaning and particle qualification.

factcards.conditions: For v > 20 m/s, dynamic run-out limit is 0.05 mm; standard RWDR fails above >0.1 mm deflection.
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not assume standard RWDR stability when dynamic deflection exceeds 0.1 mm.

factcards.conditions: ISO 21920 surface specification for PTFE lips.
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not approve a shaft without explicit Ra, Rz and Rmr evidence.

factcards.value: T_total = T_medium + DeltaT_friction; +10K doubles aging rate
factcards.conditions: Thermal load model with Arrhenius aging acceleration.
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not rate lifetime from medium temperature alone; include frictional self-heating.

factcards.conditions: Required due to abrasive filler effects in PTFE compounds.
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend filled PTFE compounds on softer shafts.

factcards.value: Prelonring BP 31: FDA/EU 1935 konform, federlos, bis 6 bar
factcards.conditions: Ideal fuer Teiganwendungen, wenn die Welle mindestens 58 HRC hat.
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend BP 31 for dough mixers if shaft hardness is below 58 HRC.

factcards.value: Bei hohem Wellenschlag (>0,2 mm) sind RWDR ungeeignet; Empfehlung: Gleitringdichtungen (z.B. EagleBurgmann SeccoMix).
factcards.conditions: Use as fallback when runout exceeds RWDR-stable operation window.
factcards.do_not_infer: Do not recommend RWDR/PTFE-lip solutions when shaft runout exceeds 0.2 mm.

gates.name: Chemical Compatibility Hard-Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Ask clarifying questions; refuse unconditional compatibility statement; propose test method ASTM D543 or supplier data.
hard_blocks: Block any statement that implies universal resistance without conditions.
assistant_actions: Return a structured question list to collect missing fields.
Flag special-case oxidizers/halogen fluorides and alkali metals as red-flag chemicals requiring specialist review and often 'not recommended'.
Recommend using rank-1 sources (ASTM tests) when stakes are high.

gates.name: Permeation / Behind-Liner Corrosion Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Ask for thickness, T, Δp and substrate; state that inert polymer can still permeate; recommend lower-permeation grades and venting.
hard_blocks: Block claims like 'PTFE ist gasdicht' or 'keine Korrosion möglich' without data.
assistant_actions: Explain the difference between permeability, permeance, and transmission rate (ASTM D1434).
If lined steel: warn about blistering/corrosion risk; suggest monitoring and design mitigations (venting, dual-liner, permeation barriers, modified PTFE).
Provide order-of-magnitude or table values only if the exact media and temperature match a cited dataset.

gates.name: Halogen Fluoride / Elemental Fluorine / ClF3 Killer Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Default to 'special-case, typically not recommended'; require specialist review and vendor confirmation; provide safe alternatives list only as 'candidates' not decisions.
hard_blocks: Block any endorsement of PTFE for ClF3/F2 at elevated temperatures without explicit certified compatibility.
assistant_actions: State that multiple sources flag deterioration/not recommended for ClF3 and elemental fluorine at high temperature.
Escalate to safety review; recommend consulting chemical supplier and performing dedicated compatibility/ignition assessment.

gates.name: Molten Alkali Metals Killer Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Default to 'not recommended'; explain attack mechanism; request confirmation of phase (molten vs aqueous).
hard_blocks: Block mixing up aqueous NaOH with molten Na/K.
assistant_actions: Explain that molten/dissolved alkali metals attack PTFE and extract fluorine (discoloration).
Ask if it is molten metal or aqueous alkali; if aqueous, fall back to standard chemical compatibility gate.

gates.name: High-Speed Rotary / PV / Heat Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Ask for missing tribology inputs; do not give wear-life predictions; propose validation test plan.
hard_blocks: Block wear-life numbers if no PV and counterface details.
assistant_actions: Compute sliding speed from rpm and diameter if provided; otherwise request.
Explain transfer-film dependency and counterface roughness effects.
Offer compound buckets (abrasive vs soft-counterface-safe) but require confirmation before final recommendation.

gates.name: Counterface Soft Metal Abrasion Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Warn against abrasive fillers (glass); steer to polymer-filled or carbon-filled options; require hardness confirmation.
hard_blocks: Block recommending glass-filled PTFE if counterface is soft/unknown.
assistant_actions: Ask for counterface hardness and finish.
Explain that some fillers are abrasive and can score shafts; propose safer filler families when soft metals are involved.

gates.name: UHP / Semiconductor Purity Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Require explicit purity specs and grade; provide only conditional guidance; encourage certification/qualification.
hard_blocks: Block claiming 'PTFE ist per se UHP'.
assistant_actions: Ask for ionic/metallic contamination limits and particle specs.
Recommend considering modified PTFE (lower permeation, less cold flow) when barrier/purity is critical, but demand data.
Highlight that vendor brochures are rank-2 and need fab qualification.

gates.name: Vacuum / Outgassing Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Explain surface vs bulk; require bake-out temperature and compatibility; warn about permeation setting base pressure.
hard_blocks: Block promises like 'UHV-ready' without outgassing/permeation data and bake-out window.
assistant_actions: Ask for bakeout temperature and allowable contaminants.
Flag decomposition hazards if bakeout near/above 300°C.

gates.name: Processing Defect: Cloudy/Opaque vs Transparent Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Differentiate between intentional opacity (filled) and defect; request density and cross-section images; propose root causes.
hard_blocks: Block diagnosing without knowing whether material is filled or virgin.
assistant_actions: Ask whether it is filled PTFE; if filled, opacity is expected.
If virgin: request density (target range) and process log; suggest CT/microscopy to confirm voids.

gates.name: Processing Defect: Blisters / Bubbles Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Treat as either trapped volatiles or permeation-driven; request whether it occurred after sintering or in service.
assistant_actions: Ask: did blisters appear right after sintering or after chemical service?
If after service: run permeation gate; if after processing: focus on lube removal, venting, compaction and heating rate.

gates.name: Agitator Rotary System Hard-Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Agitator applications require mandatory run-out, pressure direction, media consistency and shaft misalignment data before any recommendation.
hard_blocks: Block any material recommendation for agitator duty when run-out or shaft hardness data is missing.
assistant_actions: Interrupt and ask mandatory agitator questions before recommending a material.
Confirm minimum shaft hardness >=58 HRC for PTFE compounds.

gates.name: Overtemperature / Decomposition Safety Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Prioritize safety; advise immediate ventilation and industrial hygiene; avoid detailed medical advice; recommend contacting safety professionals.
hard_blocks: Block normalizing overheating; block claims that fumes are harmless.
assistant_actions: State that decomposition products can include HF and other toxic species above ~300°C; severe above ~400°C.
Recommend following SDS and workplace safety procedures; if exposure suspected, seek medical evaluation.

gates.name: Hard UHP / Semiconductor Purity Gate
gates.if_missing_fields: Halt recommendation. State that 'PTFE' is a polymer family and does not guarantee UHP. Demand extractables data (ICP-MS, IC, TOC) and particle metrics.
hard_blocks: Block any qualitative statement like 'PTFE ist sehr rein und daher geeignet' without quantitative ppb/ppt data or SEMI F57-aligned evidence.
assistant_actions: Enforce the distinction between standard commercial PTFE (possible catalyst/process residues, machining oils) and UHP-grade molded/cleaned PTFE.
Ask for analyte list, extraction protocol and whether particle shedding / surface finish was qualified.

runtime_policy.gate_version_precedence: Prefer higher semantic version suffix (e.g., PTFE-G-007-V2 over PTFE-G-007) when both match.
runtime_policy.notes: Schema v1.3 adds machine-readable required_fields_schema for gates.
